_notes: Built from AEMT SMC tracking data — Group 2.
AEMT scope builds on EMT foundations. Key additions over EMT:
- IV access (20 attempts minimum)
- IO access and IO medication administration
- IV bolus medication (10 minimum)
- IM injection (2 minimum)
- Intranasal medication (2 minimum)
- Supraglottic airway — advanced level (10 minimum)
- ETCO2 monitoring (10 minimum)
- Venous blood sampling (4 minimum, simulation permitted)
- PPV/BVM at ALS level (10 minimum)
- AED (2 minimum)
- Chest compressions (2 minimum)
- ET suction (2 minimum)
No ETI — supraglottic is the advanced airway ceiling for AEMT.
No cardiac interventions beyond AED and chest compressions.
Team leads: 10-20% of exposures (5-10) in field experience and capstone.
Program is Group 2 — building toward CoAEMSP readiness.
Scenario content not yet populated — no AEMT weekly spreadsheets parsed.
Run AEMT weekly spreadsheets through the lab parser to populate station scenario content.

templates.title: AEMT Program Orientation and EMT Skill Confirmation

stations.format_notes: Orientation to AEMT program scope, lab facilities, Platinum portal, and documentation expectations. AEMT builds on EMT — confirm EMT skill carry-over baseline.

skills.name: PPV / BVM ventilation — adult
skills.signoff_note: EMT carry-over — confirm baseline before advancing to AEMT airway scope

skills.name: Chest compressions
skills.signoff_note: CoAEMSP minimum 2 — confirm carry-over from EMT

skills.name: AED / Automated Defibrillator
skills.signoff_note: CoAEMSP minimum 2 — confirm carry-over from EMT

stations.format_notes: Verify EMT foundation skills before beginning AEMT-specific content. BVM, CPR, and AED are CoAEMSP minimums for AEMT — start the count here if not carried from EMT records.

skills.name: Establish IV access
skills.signoff_note: CoAEMSP minimum 20 total — begin accumulation. Manikin arms first, live stick introduced later. Track attempt count across all sessions.

stations.format_notes: IV access on manikin arms — technique foundation before live stick. CoAEMSP requires 20 successful IV attempts total across lab and clinical. Start tracking. Multiple rotations recommended.

skills.name: Administer IV bolus medication
skills.signoff_note: CoAEMSP minimum 10 — begin accumulation. Simulation permitted.

stations.format_notes: IV bolus technique introduced alongside IV access. Simulation permitted for CoAEMSP count. CoAEMSP minimum 10 total.
Medication math and draw practice to support IV and IM administration. SNHD protocol context.
IM injection technique. CoAEMSP minimum 2, simulation permitted. Equipment: IM injection trainers, appropriate needles and syringes.
Intranasal administration technique — MAD device. CoAEMSP minimum 2, simulation permitted. Common agents: naloxone IN, midazolam IN.

templates.title: IO Access and IO Medication Administration

stations.format_notes: IO access technique — EZ-IO or similar. CoAEMSP minimum 2, simulation permitted. Equipment: IO drill, IO needles, IO trainers (tibial and humeral sites).
IO medication administration following IO access establishment. CoAEMSP minimum 2, simulation permitted.

skills.name: Venous blood sampling
skills.signoff_note: CoAEMSP minimum 4 total — simulation permitted. Begin accumulation.

stations.format_notes: Venous blood sampling technique. CoAEMSP minimum 4, simulation permitted. Equipment: blood sampling trainers, Vacutainer system, appropriate tubes.

templates.title: IV Live Stick and IV Bolus Accumulation

skills.name: Establish IV access — live stick
skills.signoff_note: Live stick begins. CoAEMSP minimum 20 total across all sessions — track cumulative count.

stations.format_notes: Live IV stick — two stations. CoAEMSP minimum 20 requires sustained practice across clinical and lab. Track attempt count carefully.
IV bolus practice. Simulation permitted for CoAEMSP count.
Apply IV access and medication administration in patient scenario context. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.

skills.name: Insert supraglottic airway — LMA
skills.signoff_note: CoAEMSP minimum 10 total across all SGA devices — supraglottic is the AEMT advanced airway ceiling. No ETI.

skills.name: Insert supraglottic airway — King Airway
skills.signoff_note: CoAEMSP minimum 10 total — accumulate across devices and sessions

skills.name: Insert supraglottic airway — iGEL
skills.signoff_note: CoAEMSP minimum 10 total — accumulate across devices and sessions

stations.format_notes: Supraglottic airway is the AEMT advanced airway ceiling — no ETI in scope. CoAEMSP minimum 10 total across all SGA devices. Equipment: LMA, King Airway, iGEL, airway manikins. Multiple rotations — high repetition session.
BVM ventilation at ALS level — includes BVM with SGA in place. CoAEMSP minimum 10 total.
ET suction technique via SGA or in-line. CoAEMSP minimum 2, simulation permitted.

skills.name: ETCO2 monitoring
skills.signoff_note: CoAEMSP minimum 10 — begin accumulation. High volume required, continue across multiple sessions.

stations.format_notes: ETCO2 monitoring introduced. CoAEMSP minimum 10 — needs sustained accumulation across lab and clinical. Equipment: capnography-capable monitor, ETCO2 sampling lines, nasal cannula ETCO2, in-line ETCO2 adapters.
Apply ETCO2 monitoring in respiratory scenario. ETCO2 count accrues here. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.

stations.scenario_title: Cardiac Pathology — ACS / Chest Pain (AEMT scope)
stations.format_notes: Cardiac pathology scenario within AEMT scope. No cardioversion or pacing — AED and CPR only for arrest. IV access and medication administration in context. CoAEMSP Cardiac pathology minimum 6.
Skills accumulation in cardiac context. IV and ETCO2 counts continue.

stations.scenario_title: Cardiac Arrest — AED and CPR (AEMT scope)
stations.format_notes: Cardiac arrest within AEMT scope — AED, CPR, airway management, IV access. CoAEMSP Cardiac arrest minimum 4 (simulation permitted). No advanced cardiac interventions.

templates.title: Trauma Scenarios and Hemorrhage Control Review

stations.format_notes: Trauma scenario with AEMT ALS interventions — IV access, fluid resuscitation, airway management. CoAEMSP Trauma minimum 6. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.

stations.station_name: IV and Venous Blood Sampling Accumulation

skills.name: Venous blood sampling
skills.signoff_note: Accumulation — CoAEMSP minimum 4 total. Simulation permitted.

stations.format_notes: IV and venous blood sampling in trauma context.

templates.title: Respiratory Emergencies — ETCO2 and Airway Integration

stations.format_notes: Respiratory pathology scenario — SGA, BVM, ETCO2. CoAEMSP Respiratory minimum 6. ETCO2 and BVM counts accrue. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.
High-volume accumulation session for airway skills. All three CoAEMSP airway minimums accumulate here.
CVA, seizure, or altered LOC scenario. CoAEMSP Medical neurologic minimum 6. IV access and medication in context. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.
CoAEMSP Psychiatric/Behavioral minimum 6. IM or IN medication administration in context — counts accrue. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.
CoAEMSP OB delivery minimum 3 (simulation permitted). Normal uncomplicated delivery with AEMT ALS interventions. SimMom or equivalent.
CoAEMSP Distressed neonate minimum 3 (simulation permitted). Neonatal resuscitation within AEMT scope — BVM, warmth, stimulation. ALS baby manikin. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.

skills.name: PPV / BVM ventilation — neonate
skills.signoff_note: Neonatal BVM within AEMT scope. Counts toward CoAEMSP PPV/BVM minimum 10.

stations.format_notes: Neonatal BVM technique. ALS baby manikins.
CoAEMSP Other medical minimum 10. IN naloxone, IV access, airway management. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.
Other medical scenario. IV dextrose administration within AEMT scope. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.
Medication administration accumulation in medical scenario context.
Complex scenario requiring multiple AEMT interventions simultaneously — IV, airway, ETCO2, medication. Team lead role active. Content pending from weekly spreadsheets.

stations.scenario_title: Cardiac Arrest — AEMT Team Lead with AED and CPR
stations.format_notes: Team lead running cardiac arrest within AEMT scope. CoAEMSP Cardiac arrest accumulation. AED, CPR, airway, IV. Counts toward team lead minimum (10-20% of exposures).

skills.name: Establish IV access
skills.signoff_note: Final accumulation push — CoAEMSP minimum 20 total

skills.name: ETCO2 monitoring
skills.signoff_note: Final accumulation push — CoAEMSP minimum 10 total

skills.name: Insert supraglottic airway
skills.signoff_note: Final accumulation push — CoAEMSP minimum 10 total

stations.format_notes: Final skills accumulation before capstone. Confirm counts against CoAEMSP minimums before this session — identify any students below threshold.

templates.title: Capstone — Team Leader Scenarios and Final Skill Sign-offs

stations.format_notes: Student as team lead. CoAEMSP team lead minimum: 10-20% of total exposures (approximately 5-10). Counts toward field experience and capstone team lead requirements.
Student as team lead for trauma scenario. All AEMT scope interventions available. Final team lead assessment.

skills.name: Establish IV access — final count confirmation
skills.signoff_note: Verify minimum 20 total reached across all sessions and clinical

skills.name: Insert supraglottic airway — final count confirmation
Administer IV bolus medication — final count confirmation
PPV / BVM ventilation — final count confirmation
Venous blood sampling — final count confirmation
skills.signoff_note: Verify minimum 4 total reached. Simulation permitted.

skills.name: Perform endotracheal suctioning — final count confirmation
Administer IM injection — final count confirmation
Administer intranasal medication — final count confirmation
AED / Automated Defibrillator — final count confirmation
Establish IO access — final count confirmation
Administer IO medication — final count confirmation

stations.format_notes: Final CoAEMSP minimum verification before program completion. Cross-reference against SMC Summary Tracking sheet. Any student below minimum requires additional clinical encounters before completion. Team lead count: verify 10-20% of total patient exposures.
